mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity risk premium discount factor, mechanically inflating present-value valuations and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return frequency during zero-rate periods is statistically indistinguishable from unconditional base rate (65.5%).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate off a pandemic-driven peak signals accelerating labor market repair, which forward-looking equity markets price as improving corporate earnings prospects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over the trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.4% in August 2020, down sharply from 14.7% April 2020 peak
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, forward 20d SPX returns are negative in more than 40% of instances, or Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect strengthening real-economy capacity utilization and corporate output, which precede earnings upgrades and drive S&P 500 returns higher over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO rebounding from COVID trough through August 2020 vintage of 101.36
failure_condition: 60-day forward Rank IC between INDPRO trailing 3-month change and SPX returns is below 0.03 across two consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery across both durable and non-durable spending channels, which is a stronger and more reliable signal of sustained economic expansion than either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST both rising through July 2020 vintages
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST co-acceleration signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or joint-signal triggered periods show forward return hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime immediately following a pandemic-driven collapse in payrolls, monthly nonfarm payroll gains carry disproportionately high information content for near-term equity sentiment, producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns that would not be observed during a stable late-cycle expansion.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month following a prior cumulative decline exceeding 5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 140,914K August 2020 vintage, recovering from sharp 2020Q2 collapse
failure_condition: Reject if the positive PAYEMS-return relationship in recovery-regime windows is not significantly stronger (p>0.05, difference-in-means test) than in non-recovery control periods, or if 5-day forward hit rate in triggered recovery periods falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the CPI index above a threshold triggers fears of premature policy tightening or eroded real purchasing power, causing short-term risk-off selling pressure and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 259.68 August 2020 vintage, low inflation environment
failure_condition: Triggered inflation-acceleration episodes show forward 5d SPX returns positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarter-over-quarter rebound in real GDP following a historic pandemic-driven contraction signals a V-shaped recovery trajectory, which supports sustained upward repricing of equities over a quarterly horizon as growth expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 15% following a prior quarterly contraction
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value 17282.188 for 2020Q2, revised_value 19077.992, reflecting historic COVID contraction and subsequent rebound
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following the GDP rebound trigger are negative in more than 40% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous recovery in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicates that both the capital and labor sides of production are expanding together, providing a stronger confirmed signal of broad economic recovery than either indicator alone, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Both INDPRO and PAYEMS rising in the August 2020 vintage after COVID trough
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO+PAYEMS co-recovery signal fails to produce higher Rank IC than either single-series baseline model over any 2-year rolling out-of-sample window, or joint-triggered hit rate falls below 55%.
